title: The Passport (Third Amendment) Rules, 1993 (16.06.1993)
text: REGD. NO. D.L.-33004/93 The Gazette of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II—Section 3—Sub-section (i) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY No. 218] NEW DELHI, THURSDAY, JUNE 24, 1993/ASADHA 3, 1915 Separate Paging is given to this Part in order that it may be filed as a separate compilation 1 423 GI/93 (1) -- 1 of 4 -- 2 THE GAZETTE: OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II-SF,O, S (i)] MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 16th June, 1993 G.S.R. 477(E).—In exercise of the pmm conferred by Section 24 of the Passports Act, 1967 (15 of 1967), the Centrist Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Passport Rules, 1967, namely :— 1. (i) These rules may be called Passport (Third Amendment) Rules, (ii) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the official Gazette. 2. In schedule II to the Passport Rules, 1967, under the heading "Part I- Passports", in the entries against "6. Diplomatic Passports", in Column 2 in item (i)(c) for the words — "wife or official hostess or husband, as the case may be, dependent sons under 24 years and dependent unmarried daughters, dependent -- 2 of 4 -- 3 father and dependent mother of an officer referred to in (a) and (b) above, when travelling with or joining such officer at his post abroad", the words "wife or official hostess or husband, as the case may oe, dependent son, dependent unmarried, separated, divorced or widowed daughter, dependent parents of an officer referred to in (a) and (b) above when travelling with or joining such officer at his post abroad) on condition of dependent status being recognised by the Ministry", shall bo substituted. [No. VII|40lj8|89] L. K. PONAPPA, Jt. Secy., (CPV) Printed by the Manager, Govt. of India IPress, Ring Road, Maya Puri, New Delhi-l 10064 and Published by the Controller of Publications, Delhi-l 10054, 1993 -- 3 of 4 -- -- 4 of 4 --
